pages.full_text:
worked out that she would only come if there was toast. Louisa took children off to a nature park. Simon & Eve looked at Humbolt, No 2 cabin, where we will stay for a fortnight during hop harvest?
	At 11 we left, [?stopped at Ledges] & went past Chepstow Racetad. We spent the night in __Almondsbury__ - a campsite within sound of M4. [?Elevenses] at Tintern Abbey.
	[tea?]
__Friday__ On to Axbridge. Elevenses just north of Clevedon. We saw Clevedon Pier. Then we parked out of town & found a deserted path into Ladye Bay (treacherous tides) & walked along the coastal path a bit. [?Lant] Lunch in the lamb? at Axbridge. We looked at the lovely church & got back to the van just before another terrific rain shower.
	On to __Greenacres__ campsite near Compton Martin. Charles & Jenny arrived at tea time, from Germany at the end of their honey moon. John & Kaz arrived in time for supper.
pages.notes: Page continues from previous spread — opening fragment 'worked out that she would only come if there was toast' is mid-narrative. 'Chepstow Racetad' likely 'Racecourse' or 'Racetrack'. Annotation above 'At 11 we left' appears to read 'stopped at Ledges' or similar — hard to read. 'Lant' before Lunch is a crossed-out word. 'lamb?' — pub name uncertain, could be 'The Lamb'. 'Greenacres' campsite near Compton Martin is in the Chew Valley / Mendip Hills area of Somerset. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume B covers 2003–2019.

pages.full_text:
We had supper in the van, catermittent showers.
__Saturday__ John's 30th birthday
We went on a 4 mile walk. The roads near the campsite are tricky & dangerous, but we managed to reach a Roman Road to go up thru the woods, up the hill. Lots of roots & turns. Lunch at the pub near the campsite. After lunch we napped then John beat Eve by nearly 100. [?Kaylos] given John a gorgeous bomber jacket for his birthday.
	At 7 we set out on the 6 mile walk to the Pony & Trap, table booked for 9. John & Kay just made it. The rest of us were a few minutes late. Delicious meal. Lamb & choc pud. We got a taxi home.

__Sunday__
We walked next to one of the 2 lakes. John & Kaz left. Charles visited a Malvern [?friend]. Simon & Eve walked to St. Andrews Blagdon - lovely stained glass. Then we met up & Jenny bought us lunch at the Chew Magna pub. Tinker has behaved
pages.notes: Page number 115 visible in top-right corner. 'catermittent' is the diary's spelling (intermittent). 'Kaylos' — proper noun unclear, possibly a name like 'Kaylos' or 'Kay has'; could read '[?Kay has] given John...'. 'Tinker' appears to be a pet name (dog?). 'St. Andrews Blagdon' is St Andrew's Church, Blagdon, Somerset. The Pony & Trap is a well-known restaurant near Chew Magna. John's 30th birthday could help date this entry if his birth year is known. Year not determinable from this page alone; volume B covers 2003–2019.
